item.title: Warwick Pro Corvette Double Buck Bass 4 - Nirvana Black Transparent Satin Finish — $2,633.50
item.description:
$2,633.50 · Excellent · Project Music

In near mint condition (one tiny mark, pictured) this Corvette double buck as a tonal monster! 


With a wide variety of tones and a neck that seems to play itself, you just cant go wrong. It comes with the Hardcase.

* Body: US swamp ash
* Bolt-on neck: 3-Piece laminated Ovangkol
* Fretboard: Wenge
* Scale: 864 mm (34", long scale)
* Fretboard radius: 20"
* Nut width: 38.5 mm
* Just-A-Nut III Tedur nut
* 24 Nickel silver jumbo frets
* Pickups: 2 MEC MM-style humbuckers
* Active MEC 2-way electronics
* Volume control with push / pull function
* Balance, treble and bass controls
* Warwick 2-piece 3D bridge and tailpiece
* String spacing: 19 mm
* Distance E to G string: 57 mm
* Warwick machine heads
* Warwick Security Locks
* hrome hardware
* CO2-neutral production
* Finish: UV painting
* Colour: Nirvana Black Transparent Satin Finish (UV lacquer)
* Made in Germany

item.title: Fender Player Plus Jazz Bass with Maple Fretboard Olympic Pearl — $450
item.description:
$450 · Very Good · Thorsten's Gear Emporium

This is a 2024 Fender Player Plus Jazz Bass in Olympic Pearl finish.

Built with parts from the Stratosphere website.

Very good condition - no major dings. There are small scratches on the control plate and pickguard. Small marks on the headstock and body near rear pickup as pictured.

Alder Body, Neck, Bridge are from the Player Plus series.

Tuners are the new tapered model from the Player II Modified series

Pickups, Preamp, and Electronics are from the Player II Modified series (with passive tone knob!)

Hipshot Drop D Extender is installed.

Comes with light duty Fender gig bag as pictured.

Neck was taken from a Sienna Sunburst Player Plus Jazz (that’s what the serial number matches to)

9lb, 15.7oz on my postage scale.


Bass is located in Plant City but I can meet in Tampa or Lakeland

item.title: Yamaha BB2005 2010-2018 - Natural — $1,552.13
item.description:
$1,552.13 · Excellent · Ecoambit's Shop

Awesome 5 string neck through bass from Yamaha, discontinued and hard to find. Infinite sustain due to its design and the playability of Yamaha basses. This bass, a bit cheaper than the ones made in Japan, has been historically used by pros who knew about its reliability (e.g. Nick Fyffe (Jamiroquai)). In very good condition and natural finish with only some small dings (see photos) normal for a bass of its age. Sold with a new hard case from Music Store for safer shipping.

item.title: 1967 Fender Mustang Bass - Dakota Red - OHSC — $5,956.56
item.description:
$5,956.56 · Very Good · Andy Baxter Bass & Guitars Ltd

1967 Fender Mustang Bass in very good condition, with a Dakota Red finish, a maple neck, and a Rosewood fingerboard. All original and complete, including the case. 

It has a great-playing straight neck, and the frets are in superb condition. The body finish has a few small scuffs and dings and light playwear, mainly around the edges. The back of the neck also has playwear through the finish. The headstock is clean with just a few small dings and some lacquer checking. Everything remains structurally sound with no breaks or repairs. The truss rod works correctly without issue. All electronics work and sound great.

Fully set up in our workshop with a set of D'Addario round-wound strings. The pots both date to the 31st week of 1966. The neck stamp reads Nov 66 B. The pickups date to Jan 67. Serial: 192052. Weight: 7 lbs 4.5 oz / 3.31 kg. Nut width: 41 mm. Depth at 1st fret: 21.5 mm.

item.title: Rickenbacker 4001CS Chris Squire Signature Bass #763/1000 – Original Case & COA – Late Production — $7,044.27
item.description:
$7,044.27 · Very Good · Javier's Gear Bazaar

For sale: my Rickenbacker 4001CS Chris Squire Limited Edition Signature Bass, serial number 763 of only 1,000 ever made. This is a late-production example (1997–2000 run), complete with the original Rickenbacker silver hardshell case and certificate of authenticity.

About this instrument: The 4001CS is one of the most historically significant production basses Rickenbacker ever built — a tribute to Chris Squire's legendary modified 1964 Rickenbacker 4001S, the bass that defined the sound of progressive rock. Only 1,000 were handcrafted between 1991 and 2000. Each came numbered, certified, and cased. No reissue has been made. None ever will.

Chris Squire passed away in 2015. Since then, the collectible value of this instrument has only increased. This is not a guitar that gets easier to find with time.

Specs:

  • Body: Maple, Cresting Wave
  • Neck: Through-body maple
  • Fingerboard: African Vermilion with dot inlays (no binding)
  • Pickups: Toaster (neck) + Horseshoe (bridge) — original
  • Output: Mono
  • Finish: Cream Colorglo
  • Hardware: Chrome, original throughout
  • Includes: Original Rickenbacker silver vintage case + Certificate of Authenticity
  • Production number: #763 / 1,000

Condition: 7.5–8/10 — Very Good The bass shows normal signs of play wear consistent with its age. All electronics, hardware, and components are 100% original. Neck is straight, frets have life left, no cracks or structural issues. Plays and sounds exactly as it should. Honest photos available on request.

Priced at €6,000. Serious inquiries only. Ship worldwide at buyer's expense and risk (insured). Based in Spain.

item.title: 1970 Fender Telecaster Bass - Blonde - OHSC — $7,393.14
item.description:
$7,393.14 · Good · Andy Baxter Bass & Guitars Ltd

1970 Fender Telecaster Bass in good + condition, Blonde finish, maple neck and fingerboard. Serial: 296814. All original and complete, including the case. 

The finish has a number of small scuffs and dings as well as some arm wear on the edge of the upper bout and a patch of wear on the edge of the lower horn. The neck and fretboard are clean overall with only minor playwear. There are a few small scuffs and dings around the top edge of the headstock. Everything remains structurally sound, with no breaks or repairs. The truss rod works correctly without issue. All electronics work and sound great. It has a great playing straight neck, and the frets are in good condition.

Fully set up in our workshop with a medium-low action and a set of Ernie Ball strings. The pots both date to 1966.  Weight: 10 lbs 3 oz / 4.62 kg. Nut width: 44.5 mm. Depth at 1st fret: 23 mm.

item.title: ESP LTD EC-414FM 2012 - Black transparent maple flame — $775
item.description:
$775 · Very Good · Mez

Up for sale is a powerhouse 2012 ESP LTD EC-414FM 4-string electric bass in a striking See-Thru Black finish over a flamed maple top. This model perfectly adapts ESP’s iconic Eclipse single-cutaway guitar shape into a sleek, aggressive, and incredibly balanced bass.Built in 2012 under the oversight of World Musical Instruments (WMI) at their respected Indonesian facility, this 400-series bass delivers professional-grade hardware and electronics at a fraction of the cost of an E-II or ESP Original. Includes the original factory ESP fitted hard shell case.

  • Cosmetic: Very Good condition. The front looks stunning, and the finish showcases the flame maple top beautifully. The only notable cosmetic flaw is one small dent on the back side of the body (captured clearly in the photos). Aside from that, there is only typical light surface swirling and minor hardware patina expected from a well-loved instrument.
  • Functional: 100% fully operational. The truss rod turns smoothly, frets have plenty of life left, and all active electronics operate quietly without scratchy pots.
  • Premium Set-Thru Neck: The 5-piece maple/walnut neck utilizes an extra-thin U contour. The set-thru construction gives you effortless access to all 22 extra-jumbo frets and creates massive, natural sustain.
  • Massive Active Tone: Outfitted with dual active EMG 35DC humbuckers paired with an onboard 3-band active EQ. The pickup blend control allows you to sweep seamlessly between a punchy bridge growl and a deep, thumping neck presence.

Full Specifications:

  • Model: LTD EC-414FM STBLK
  • Year: 2012 (Serial: IW12023027)
  • Country of Origin: Indonesia (WMI / PT Cort Factory)
  • Body Wood: Mahogany with Flamed Maple Top
  • Neck Wood: 5-piece Maple/Walnut (Set-Thru construction)
  • Fretboard: Rosewood, 400mm radius, flag inlays
  • Scale Length: 34"
  • Pickups: Active EMG 35DC (Bridge & Neck)
  • Controls: Master Volume, Balance, 3-Band Active EQ (Bass/Mid/Treble)
  • Hardware: Black Nickel
  • Case: Genuine ESP Factory Hard Shell Case Included
  • Pro-Tier Hardware: Features rock-solid Grover tuners and an ESP BB-604 bridge to ensure rock-solid tuning stability, no matter how hard you dig in.

Shipping Info:
Ships promptly and securely packed inside its factory hard case, which will be safely boxed for transit. The 9V battery will be safely disconnected inside the control cavity prior to shipping to protect the electronics during transit.Please feel free to reach out with any questions or reasonable offers! Thanks for looking.

item.title: 【used】AKAI / Deep Impact SB1【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $903
item.description:
$903 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

AKAI is a brand that has released many pieces of equipment, primarily audio equipment.


This model is a legendary bass synthesizer among AKAI's many products.


While its successor, the Future Impact, is a successor to the Deep Impact, the sound is different when you compare them side-by-side.


The Deep Impact has a narrower range, resulting in a denser and fuller sound.


A legendary piece of equipment that is indispensable when discussing synth pedals.


Don't miss this opportunity.


There are some scratches and stains, and it shows signs of use. It is in good working order.


GIB's craftsmen will rigorously inspect the item before delivery.


Accessories: None

item.title: 【new】moon / JJ-5 Larry Graham model BK 4.390kg #LG0083【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $4,244
item.description:
$4,244 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Moon is a leading, long-established guitar brand in Japan.

Highly regarded by many professional artists, it's well-known that Mitsuru Sudo and Larry Graham are users of the brand.

This JJ model, with its slanted front pickup, is highly regarded as a flagship model.

This particular model is a signature model of legendary funk bassist Larry Graham.

Although a signature model, it's also known to be used by top-tier bassists such as Pino Ppalladino.

In fact, Pino Ppalladino used this black color model extensively in recordings and live performances.

These facts demonstrate that this model is not merely an artist model, but rather its appeal lies purely in its high quality.

The body is made of ash, and the neck and fretboard are made of maple, a typical Moon construction.

Notable features include the Bartolini 9s pickup and the NTBT preamp, which is unusual for a JB-style bass.

You'll get a more explosively powerful, deep, and crisp sound.

GOTOH pickups are used for the bridge and tuners, carefully chosen for their weight and other factors.

The sound, with its sharp, crisp tone during slap and fingerstyle playing, and rich low end, is particularly noteworthy.

I believe this sound is why it has been loved by bassists for so long.

●SPECS

Body: Ash 3p
Neck: Maple
Fingerboard: Maple
Pickups: Bartolini 9S5 & NTBT
Parts: Gold
Color: Black
etc: Urethane Finish

GIB's craftsmen will adjust the instrument for optimal playability before delivery.

Accessories: Hard case, hex wrench, warranty card

Inspection 담당: Higashie

Product photos: Higashie

item.title: 【used】Sadowsky / MX24 MS5 CAO/R 2025 3.880kg #A001316-25【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,270
item.description:
$1,270 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Sadowsky is a high-end New York brand with top-tier musicians like Marcus Miller and Will Lee as clients.


Previously, the Metroline Express was manufactured in Japan, but as Warwick took over distribution of Sadowsky products, manufacturing shifted to a factory in China.


While keeping the selling price down, high quality is maintained, making it a very unique product in its price range.


Initially, the Metroline Express series did not have the Sadowsky logo, but this has now been changed.


This is a 24-fret modern model.


The body is made of Okoume, the neck is made of Roasted Maple, and the fingerboard is made of Morado (Pau Ferro).


The body balance and sound are well thought out, resulting in a very refined and balanced finish.


The neck has a satin finish, giving it a smooth feel.


The pickups are soapbar type, and the preamp is the familiar 2-band boost EQ type.


The preamp circuit board is almost identical to that of top-of-the-line models priced around 1 million yen, which I think is a significant advantage.


The controls are volume, balance, bass, and treble.


A push-pull switch allows you to bypass the active circuit and play passively.


The powerful and crisp sound sounds a little more flamboyant than recent NYC models.


Some people might even prefer this sound based on the tone alone.


It seems difficult to produce a product of the same high quality at the same price point.


Highly recommended.


The frets have approximately 80% remaining.


The truss rod is in good condition.


The neck is in good condition.


The body and paintwork show a 6mm paint chip next to the neck pickup. There are also some minor scratches.


The electronics are in good condition.


No obvious modifications were observed.


Controls: Volume, Balancer, Bass (Push-Pull / Active-Passive), Treble

Nut Width: 47.7mm

Bridge String Spacing: 18.0mm

Scale Length: 34 Inch

Year of Manufacture: 2025

Country of Manufacture: CHINA


During setup, we use our original GIB BASIC / Standard Bass Strings.


These strings are characterized by their straightforward sound and stable playability, naturally bringing out the instrument's inherent character.


These are highly reliable strings that we actually use in our repair and setup workshops.


Please enjoy these strings, which aim to be a new standard for bassists.


Delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.


Accessories: Gig bag, wrench, strap pins

item.title: Valley Arts USED ​​M-Series JB Type — $741.36
item.description:
$741.36 · Good · Ikebe Music

Our Business Hours is Mon to Fri / 11:00 to 18:00 JPN time
We will close Sat and Sun, so we will reply to inquiry that received weekend on next week.
Your understanding will be appreciated.

We are receiving the customer order with first comes first serves basis both on website and at real shop.
So occasionally it will be sold out while having email communication.
Please understand in advance.

Japanese consumption tax is not required for international shopping. However you may be required to pay duty, tax and import fee when receiving your items in your country. You must pay all these duty, tax and fee as receiver/importer and we do not cover any of them.

■Please note
The product description is an automatic translation of the Japanese description. Please contact us if you have any questions.

-----------------------------------

Please be noted this one is USED not new.
There is not any issue on the function and the playing condition but blemishes on the cosmetic.
Please send message for more detail information of the condition.

-----------------------------------

A rare used bass model from Valley Arts, a brand that remains highly popular, has arrived!

This guitar features two pickups that appear to be dual-coil. The combination of an ash body and likely an ebony fingerboard results in a crisp response and a punchy sound.
The controls consist of front volume, rear volume, treble, and bass, and the mini-switches appear to be for front series/parallel, rear series/parallel, Notch On/Off, and Kill SW respectively.

Details are unclear, but it currently has a preamplifier labeled SB1.
There are some minor scratches and dents, but it is currently in good working order and can be played without any problems.
The truss rod has plenty of adjustment range, and the neck condition is good with no major issues.

[Slightly Damaged - Special Price]

Markbass, a globally renowned bass amplifier manufacturer, presents the Kimandu electric bass from its GLOXY series!

The GLOXY series employs carefully selected components and materials to achieve excellent cost performance.

This eye-catching model, with its unique original shape, is a signature model developed in collaboration with Richard Bona. The 24-fret maple neck, finished with a flat radius, is bolted onto the body with a deeply cut joint, reliably meeting the demands of players with its deep sustain, crisp attack, and ease of playability in the higher positions. The standard wood materials of alder body and maple neck/fretboard produce a rich, wide-range sound, which is faithfully reproduced by the soapbar-type pickups, adding thickness to the tone.

Equipped with the same MB INSTRUMENT Pre found in higher-end models, the tone control not only boosts/cuts specific frequency ranges but also ensures that the entire frequency range, from low to high, is preserved even at extreme settings. Furthermore, the design minimizes volume differences when switching between passive and active modes, making it a practical choice.

*There is a paint defect on the body top, but it does not affect playability.

The product description is an automatic translation of the Japanese description. Please contact us if you have any questions.

-----------------------------------

The Tradmaster series features a lacquer and aged finish!

BLACK SMOKER is a handmade brand from BLACK CLOUD, a workshop in Nagano Prefecture, a mecca for musical instrument manufacturing where many brands are located. This brand, which is already generating buzz among many professional musicians for its high sense of style and high quality in terms of sound, playability, and looks, is a noteworthy brand created by three people: Mr. Kuroiwa, the representative who has been involved in the design and manufacture of numerous manufacturers and models; Mr. Yanagisawa, a young builder who creates with him; and Mr. Itasaka, a builder who has honed his skills at a long-established Japanese brand.

The "Trad Master" series has already gained high praise and popularity. This model features a simple yet standard, classic specification that allows you to fully appreciate the brand's true value, finished with lacquer paint and aged processing to give it a vintage feel. It adopts standard specifications based on the 60s style, with an alder body, maple neck, and rosewood fingerboard. Although the specifications are simple, you will immediately know that the materials have been carefully selected from the first note you play. The neck shape has a solid nut width and thickness typical of a PB, but the smooth curve makes it easy to play. The original pickups reproduce the resonance of the body while producing a thick, punchy sound. The setup is smooth and stress-free to play. In short, this bass is a product where the "quality of the instrument's construction" is directly conveyed. The tonal inconsistency that is a problem with many PB-type basses is settled in just the right place, "retaining some wildness while being well-controlled," resulting in a wonderful finish that fuses the wildness of a PB with modern ease of use at a high level. The aged lacquer finish, which exudes exceptional taste, along with the aged parts (which, like the pickups, have been modified specifically for this series) and the vintage-style bridge, contribute significantly not only to the looks but also to a thicker, more impactful sound.

In terms of appearance, it boasts a meticulously crafted design, including an aged finish with minimal weather checking and an exquisitely nuanced Olympic White color. While maintaining a vintage-like look, the playability and sound pressure are undeniably impressive, reflecting the brand's pride in its construction. This is a truly superb bass that I wholeheartedly recommend, achieving at an extremely high level the ideal of "a coexistence of vintage looks and sound with modern playability and stability."

Due to its limited production run as a handmade instrument, this highly-regarded brand has very limited availability. With playability and sound recognized by professionals, this bass is already making a name for itself in the music scene. Experience its exceptional quality for yourself.

Body Wood: Alder
Neck Wood: Maple
Fingerboard Wood: Rosewood
Neck Shape: C Shape
Scale: 864 scale/20F
Nut/Width: Bone/42mm
Frets: Medium Jumbo
Radius: 250R
HW Color: Chrome & Nickel
Machineheads: GOTOH GB528
Bridge: 4-saddle Vintage Style
Pickguard: 4-Ply Red Tortoiseshell
Pickups: BLACK SMORKER
Electronics: Volume, Tone
Neck Finish: Lacquer
Body Finish: Lacquer
Color: Olympic White /Light Aged

item.description:
$1,176 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Fujigen is a world-renowned guitar factory in Japan.


Its quality is highly regarded globally, and it has manufactured OEM parts for many guitar brands, including Fender, G&L, and Ibanez.


FGN is the original brand of this world-class guitar factory.


This model is a 5-string Jazz Bass type with an orthodox style that feels somehow new.


The body is made of alder and the fretboard is rosewood, a classic wood combination.


The specifications evoke a 1960s Fender.


The frets utilize the brand's signature CFS (circle fretting system).


CFS uses a fan-shaped fret arrangement, creating a right-angle angle to the strings and resulting in more accurate scale setting.


The controls are simple: neck pickup volume, bridge pickup volume, and master tone.


While keeping costs to a minimum, this is a masterpiece that highlights their high level of technical skill.


We highly recommend this bass to anyone looking for their first 5-string bass or someone struggling to choose their second bass.


The frets have approximately 85% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck has a slight warp and a slight buzzing sound in the lower positions on the treble side. (This is not noticeable when amplified.) PLEK tuning is recommended for optimal performance.

The body and paintwork have scratches throughout.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

Rock N Roll Relics is a guitar/bass brand based in California, USA.


They primarily specialize in traditional styles and are particularly highly regarded for their finishes.


They are known to be used by musicians such as Billie Joe Armstrong (Green Day) and Ryan Roxie.


This model is a replica of a JB bass.


The body is made of alder, and the fingerboard is rosewood, a classic construction.


The lacquer finish and clay dots show respect for vintage instruments.


The pickups are D. Allen Pickups CS-J Bass.


It has simple passive controls.


The powerful low end and punchy high frequencies are truly classic.


It will inspire the player both in looks and sound.


The frets have approximately 85% remaining.


The truss rod is in good condition.


The neck is in good condition.


Due to the relic finish, it is difficult to distinguish between new and used scratches on the body and finish.


The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

Controls: Neck pickup volume, bridge pickup volume, tone

Nut width: 39.1mm

Bridge string spacing: 19.0mm

Scale length: 34 inches

Year of manufacture: 2014

Country of manufacture: USA


During setup, we used D'addario EXL strings, highly trusted by professional musicians.


Characterized by a powerful attack and rich harmonics, these strings truly deserve to be called a standard.


These high-quality strings bring out the instrument's performance and character to the fullest, so please enjoy them as well.


GIB's craftsmen will adjust the instrument for easy playability before delivery.


Accessories: Semi-hard case, finger rest, spec sheet

item.title: 【used】Spector / NS-JH5FM Natural Oil 2003 3.900kg #164【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $4,470
item.description:
$4,470 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Spector is a guitar and bass brand started in 1974 in New York by Stuart Spector.


Initially, Vinny Fodera, who would later found Fodera Guitars, also worked at the Spector workshop.


The brand's NS models gained explosive popularity worldwide, and these were created in collaboration with Ned Steinberger.


This particular instrument is an NS-JH, a very rare bolt-on neck model from their top-of-the-line USA-made line.


The body back is likely alder, and the top features beautifully figured quilted maple.


The neck is 3-piece maple, and the fingerboard appears to be rosewood.


The pickups are EMG, featuring a distinctive single-coil + humbucker configuration.


The preamp is original, a classic combination.


The scale length is 35 inches.


This guitar boasts a perfectly balanced sound with rich, resonant bass and clear, vibrant treble, producing a flawless tone for backing, chord work, and solo playing.


This is an extremely rare instrument, produced in very limited quantities.


Recommended not only for Spector fans, but also for players who love modern guitars like Fodera, MTD, and Ken Smith.


Fret life is approximately 70%.


The truss rod is in good condition.


The neck is in good condition.


The body and paintwork show signs of use, with scattered scratches and dents. There is a 50.0mm scratch on the body top, resulting in paint chipping. There are also areas near the neck pickup where the wood has been worn down by approximately 15.0mm and 30.0mm.


The electronics are in good condition.


The strap pins have been extended. The bridge pickup mounting screws have been replaced. The end pin position has been changed, and a screw hole is present where the original was.

Controls: Neck pickup volume, Bridge pickup volume (push/pull - bridge pickup single coil/bridge pickup humbucker)

Nut width: 45.5mm

String spacing at bridge: 16.0mm

Scale length: 35 inches

Year of manufacture: 2003

Country of manufacture: USA


During setup, we use D'addario EXL strings, highly trusted by professional musicians.


Characterized by a powerful attack and rich harmonics, these strings truly deserve to be called a standard.


These high-quality strings bring out the maximum performance and character of the instrument, so please enjoy them as well.


GIB's craftsmen will adjust the instrument for easy playability before delivery.


Accessories: Original hard case, lock pins, strap


Text: Ryoma Terao


Inspection 담당: Hirayama


Product Photos: Tei

item.title: 【new】Sadowsky / MX21 HP4 OWT/M 3.710kg #F000716-25【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,369
item.description:
$1,369 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Sadowsky is a high-end New York brand with top musicians like Marcus Miller and Will Lee as clients.


Previously, the Metroline Express was manufactured in Japan, but as Warwick took over the distribution of Sadowsky products, manufacturing moved to a factory in China.


While keeping the selling price down, high quality is maintained, making it a very unique product in its price range.


Initially, the Metroline Express series did not have the Sadowsky logo, but this has now been changed.


Furthermore, the neck has been changed to roasted maple.


It has a satin finish and a smooth feel.


The back panel is a screwless type, similar to Warwick.


Looking inside the cavity, you can see that it is shielded with copper foil tape. It's wonderful.


The pickups are PJ type, with a Precision Bass style in the neck position and a Jazz Bass style in the bridge position.


The bridge pickup is a hum-canceling type, minimizing the noise often associated with PJ pickups. The preamp circuit board is almost identical to that of top-of-the-line models priced around 1 million yen, which I think is a significant advantage.


The controls are volume, balance, bass, and treble.


A push-pull switch allows you to bypass the active circuit and play passively.


The powerful and crisp sound sounds a little more flamboyant than recent NYC models.


Some people might even prefer this sound based on the tone alone.


It seems that creating a product with the same high quality at the same price point is not easy.


Highly recommended.


●SPECS


4-String Electric Bass

Scale Length: 34"""" (864 mm)

Bolt-On Construction

Body Wood: Okoume

Neck Wood: Roasted Maple

Fingerboard: Roasted Maple

Fingerboard Radius: 12""""

Nut: Sadowsky Just-A-Nut III

Nut Width: 1.54"" (39.2 mm)

Frets: 21 Nickel Silver (width: 0.094"""" (2.4 mm) / height: 0.051"""" (1.3 mm))

Neck Pickup: Passive Sadowsky P-Style

Bridge Pickup: Passive hum-cancelling Sadowsky J-Style in 60s position

Electronics: Sadowsky Active 2-Way

Controls: Volume / Balance / Treble / Bass

Push/pull function at Bass knob for preamp bypass

Tuners: Sadowsky Light machine heads with open gear

Security Locks: Sadowsky S-style

Bridge System: Sadowsky bridge with quick string release

Hardware Color: Chrome

Strings: 045 - 105 Stainless Steel

Included Accessories: Sadowsky Fretboard Protector and Hex Key Tools

Gig Bag: Sadowsky Portabag (SAC BAG BASS PORTA)


Delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.


Accessories: Original gig bag, warranty card
